assertions.expectation: Distinguishes between dev (Rapid) and prod (Stable) clusters in the checklists
assertions.reason: Response explicitly mentions testing on dev clusters first and coordinating maintenance windows for prod, recognizing the different channels

assertions.expectation: Recognizes Autopilot limitations (no node management, no SSH, mandatory resource requests)
assertions.reason: Includes dedicated Autopilot-specific section mentioning no node management, workload rescheduling concerns, and limited customization

assertions.expectation: Includes API deprecation checks with kubectl commands
assertions.reason: Mentions checking deprecated APIs and provides specific kubectl command: 'kubectl get events --field-selector reason=FailedMount'

assertions.expectation: Mentions mandatory resource requests as an Autopilot-specific concern
assertions.reason: Specifically mentions 'ensure proper resource requests/limits' in Autopilot notes and 'Review resource quotas' in pre-flight checks

assertions.expectation: Pre-upgrade checklist includes workload readiness items (PDBs, bare pods, graceful shutdown)
assertions.reason: Does not explicitly mention PodDisruptionBudgets, bare pods identification, or graceful shutdown configuration

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes version verification commands
assertions.reason: Mentions verifying cluster status but doesn't provide specific kubectl commands to verify the actual version upgrade

assertions.expectation: Post-upgrade checklist includes workload health validation
assertions.reason: Includes comprehensive workload validation: deployments/services health, application endpoints, resource usage monitoring

assertions.expectation: Recommends testing on dev clusters before prod auto-upgrade
assertions.reason: First item in pre-upgrade checklist explicitly states 'Test on dev clusters first - Upgrade your Rapid channel dev clusters and validate all workloads'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that checking the GKE release schedule gives ~1 month best-case advance notice for new minor upgrades, beyond the 72h notification
assertions.reason: Does not mention the GKE release schedule or the ~1 month advance notice capability
Multiple monitoring items included: verify monitoring/alerting is active, monitor application metrics, check resource usage patterns

assertions.expectation: Does NOT proactively recommend node-level maintenance exclusions for Autopilot unless the customer explicitly asks for node upgrade control
assertions.reason: Correctly does not mention maintenance exclusions, appropriately noting that Google handles node management in Autopilot
